message:
feat(wezterm+sharding): WeztermInterface trait activate_pane_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extend the `WeztermInterface` trait with a Cx-first `activate_pane`
entry point, parallel to the existing `list_panes_with_cx`,
`get_pane_with_cx`, `send_text*_with_cx`, etc. Without this
extension, calling a trait-object's `_with_cx` for activate would
land on a trait default that falls back to the non-cx `activate_pane`
at the wrapper boundary (see tick 30/31 notes).

Additions (all strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `WeztermInterface::activate_pane_with_cx` trait default —
    delegates to `self.activate_pane(pane_id)` for backwards
    compatibility. Concrete impls with a Cx-aware
    `wezterm cli activate-pane` path SHOULD override.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for WeztermClient` override — routes
    through the inherent `WeztermClient::activate_pane_with_cx`
    landed in tick 40.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for Arc<dyn WeztermInterface>` override —
    forwards `cx` through `self.as_ref().activate_pane_with_cx(cx,
    pane_id)` so the trait-object boundary does not drop cx. Without
    this, the trait default would call `self.as_ref().activate_pane`
    (non-cx).

  * `impl WeztermInterface for UnifiedClient` override — routes
    through `self.inner.activate_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id)`.

  * `impl WeztermInterface for ShardedWeztermClient` override (in
    `crates/frankenterm-core/src/sharding.rs`) — routes pane_id
    through `route_for_global_pane_id_with_cx` then dispatches to
    `backend.handle.activate_pane_with_cx(cx, route.local_pane_id)`
    with the same backend_error wrapping as the other sharded Cx
    overrides. Keeps the Sharded hop cx-aware.

Regression test (new)
`activate_pane_with_cx_forwards_through_arc_and_unified`:

  * Builds a WeztermClient with a bogus socket path (temp dir +
    PID + nanos so it is guaranteed not to exist).
  * Wraps in `Arc<dyn WeztermInterface>`, invokes the trait's
    `activate_pane_with_cx(&cx, 99)`, asserts the result is
    `Wezterm(SocketNotFound(path))` with the exact bogus path
    preserved.
  * Builds a `UnifiedClient::from_handle(arc, BackendSelection::Cli)`,
    invokes again, asserts the same `SocketNotFound` surface.

This pins the contract that the tick-44 wrapper overrides route
through to the concrete `WeztermClient::activate_pane_with_cx`
short-circuit. If any of the overrides regressed to the trait
default (silently dropping cx through the wrapper hop), we would
still observe SocketNotFound in today's codebase but a future
concrete override with different pre-flight behavior would diverge —
this test would catch the drift.

Existing tick 40 test `activate_pane_with_cx_short_circuits_on_missing_socket`
(direct WeztermClient path) continues to run — together the two
tests prove the full call chain:
  trait `_with_cx` on Arc<dyn> / UnifiedClient
  -> concrete WeztermClient `_with_cx`
  -> `run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx` (tick 40 helper)
  -> `run_cli_with_cx` (tick 40 helper)

Verification (local fork bypass, CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):
  * wezterm:: — 167 passed / 0 failed (tick 43: 166 -> tick 44: 167,
    +1 new test)
  * sharding:: — 95 passed / 0 failed (unchanged count, new override
    compiles + all existing tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (44s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Next follow-on slices on this trail: parallel trait extensions for
`kill_pane_with_cx`, `zoom_pane_with_cx`, `spawn_with_cx`,
`spawn_targeted_with_cx`, `split_pane_with_cx` — each is mechanical
now that tick 44 established the pattern on activate.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
